data.title: CLI_user tag for user-reported bugs with Gate 1a intent search skip
data.description:
## Context
DSO plugin users who report bugs during interactive Claude Code sessions are penalized by the fix-bug pipeline's Gate 1a intent search. The intent search dispatches a sonnet sub-agent that spends ~20 tool calls searching closed tickets, git history, ADRs, and code comments to determine whether the bug aligns with system intent — a waste when the user explicitly asked for the bug to be filed. Worse, the intent search can classify user-reported bugs as "intent-contradicting" and auto-close them as "working as designed," overriding the user's explicit judgment.

## Success Criteria
1. ticket create supports a --tags flag that sets tags atomically at creation time
2. When the agent creates a bug ticket on behalf of the user during interactive conversation, the bug is tagged CLI_user
3. /dso:fix-bug Gate 1a skips the intent-search agent dispatch for bugs tagged CLI_user, recording GATE_1A_RESULT="intent-aligned" directly
4. When /dso:debug-everything processes a CLI_user-tagged bug in bug-fix mode, the inlined fix-bug execution records GATE_1A_RESULT="intent-aligned" without dispatching the intent-search sub-agent
5. Bugs created autonomously by sub-agents (discovered defects during sprint/debug-everything execution) do NOT carry the CLI_user tag, and Gate 1a intent-search dispatches normally for untagged bugs

## Scenario Analysis
- Reducer silently discards tags in CREATE event (critical, deployment): The reducer CREATE handler does not read tags from data — it initializes state["tags"] = []. Tags at creation are silently dropped unless the CREATE handler is updated to read data.get('tags', []).
- debug-everything has no tag-check step (high, runtime): debug-everything bug-fix mode loop does not extract tags from ticket show output before inlining fix-bug, so the CLI_user check has no TAGS variable in scope at Step 1.5.

## Dependencies
Soft coordination with 0d66-ceb6 (Unified Primary Ticket Model) — if that epic restructures ticket create internals, --tags implementation should target the post-refactor interface. Soft coordination with 28cb-a23a (skill nesting elimination) — if that epic changes how debug-everything inlines fix-bug, SC4 should target the post-refactor invocation path. Neither is a hard blocker.

## Approach
Add --tags flag to ticket-create.sh (arg parsing + inclusion in CREATE event JSON). Update the ticket reducer CREATE handler to read tags from data.get('tags', []). Update fix-bug SKILL.md Step 1.5 to check for CLI_user tag before dispatching intent-search. Update debug-everything bug-fix mode loop to extract tags from ticket show output before inlining fix-bug. Add guidance in CLAUDE.md and agent instruction files for when to apply the CLI_user tag. The tag means "a human explicitly requested this bug be filed" — it bypasses intent search but does not bypass investigation. If investigation reveals the behavior is working as designed, the agent escalates to the user rather than auto-closing.
